data.description:
## Why
Bug d843 bounds the ACLI subprocess. But the GIT network ops have the same unbounded-hang exposure and are OUTSIDE the acli path: _store/sync.py:116 `git fetch origin tickets` and _store/push.py `git push` (and _attestation.py:107 `git verify-commit`, which can hang on gpg-agent/keyserver) all call subprocess.run with NO timeout. A stuck remote or gpg-agent freezes the write/sync/attestation path.

## What
- Add bounded `timeout=` to the network git subprocesses: _store/sync.py `_ok`/fetch path, _store/push.py `_run`/push path. Treat timeout as best-effort failure (these are already best-effort) — never hang.
- Add a timeout to _attestation.py:107/118 git verify-commit (gpg-agent hang).
- These are local/simple git calls (no pipe-holding grandchildren), so a plain subprocess.run(timeout=) suffices — NOT the acli process-group treatment.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] git fetch (sync.py) + git push (push.py) have a bounded timeout; a stuck remote does not hang the caller (best-effort failure preserved)
- [ ] git verify-commit (_attestation) bounded against gpg-agent hang
- [ ] Unit test: a stubbed slow git command surfaces a bounded timeout
- [ ] Suite green; ruff clean
data.title: Network git subprocesses (fetch/push/verify-commit) have no timeout
